coach_notes:
- Scale the L-Sit to get a 1-minute accumulated Hold within 2-3 mins
- Target for fast athletes is under 45 minutes, 50 mins is still very good, under 60 minutes should be possible for most
- Beginners can scale all the movements and distances
- It should take between 2-3 minutes for the Up-Downs
- follow the order of exercises except for Rower/SkiErg/Airbike which can be done in any order


### Previous scores as reference:

### Conversions
For the Rogue Echo Bike:
1000 metres = 0.62 miles
2000 metres = 1.24 miles
4000 metres = 2.48 miles

Row - Airbike - Run Conversion
100 meters Row =  0.13 miles (200 metres) on the Rogue Echo Bike.
It takes an average athlete 24 seconds to row 100M. (4 mins per 1000m)
Rowing 100M burns about 6 calories.
Biking 0.13 miles (200 metres) burns about 4.2 calories.

10 calories Row = 7 calories on the Rogue Echo Bike.
It takes an average athlete 36 seconds to row 10 calories.
10 Rowing Calories is equivalent to 166 meters rowed.
7 Calories on the Echo Bike is about 0.22 miles (354 metres)

In two minutes most people can row 500 meters.
In three minutes most people can Airbike 1 Mile (1600 metres)

10 Calories Rower = 7 Calories Echo Bike
500M Row takes 2 Minutes
1000 Meters Row = 1.3 Miles (2092 metres) on Echo Bike
1 Mile (1600 metres) AirBike takes 3 Minutes

Approx conversions:
1 mile run (1600 metres) = 2000 metres Row = 2.6 miles (4184 metres)
1/2 mile run (800 metres = 1000 metres Row = 1.3 miles (2092 metres)
1/4 mile run (400 metres = 500 metres Row = 0.64 miles (1046 metres)

WOD Explanation:
2 rounds of 6 movements:
1 min per movement, 15 seconds change over
2 mins REST between rounds

* Pull-Up Strict
* Parallettes L-Sit Hold

* KB OH Carry
2 x KB held overhead

Put your total score here

coach_notes:
- Focus today is on strict precise movement, no kipping, jerking or swinging
- If any exercise is too easy, we add weight, if too hard, we scale it to make it easier
- Class is planned for 1 hour plus stretching which athletes can do alone


### Push-ups Strict (6 mins)

**PoP:**
- Straight line from shoulders to heels, no raising or sagging in the hips
- Head/neck in neutral position, do not lower or raise the head
- Feet together, touching
- Hands underneath and slightly outside the shoulders
- Elbows go backwards diagonally, not out to the sides
- On Timer & Coach's cue

### Strict Push-Ups
Each rep should last 10 seconds, 5 seconds down, 5 seconds up
Min 1: 6 x Push-Ups in 60 secs
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 6 x Weighted Push-Ups in 60 secs
Scale up or down as needed but try and work for the full 60 seconds

Push-Ups scale up: add weight plate on upper back
Choose weight to make it as difficult as possible but make sure you can complete the 6 reps within 60 secs

Push-Ups Scale down:
Sc1: Strict negative, knees down, push-up, raise knees to start position
Sc2: Begin on knees and perform as above
If 6 reps cannot be completed with good form on Sc2, reduce the reps to 5 or 4 accordingly


### Pull-Ups Strict chin over bar (6 mins)
Same format as above where we add weight to make it more difficult or scale it to make it easier
Min 1: 6 x Strict Pull-Ups in 60 seconds (IGYG with partner)
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 6 x Strict Weighted Pull-Ups in 60 seconds (IGYG with partner)
Pull-Ups scale up: add weight plate to weight belt, 
Choose weight to make it as difficult as possible but make sure you can complete the 6 reps within 60 secs

Pull-Ups Scale down:
Banded Pull-Ups - every rep starts from a static position, no bouncing, swinging or kipping


### Parallettes L-Sit Hold (6 mins)
2 rounds of:
Min 1: 10-20 secs unbroken Parallettes L-Sit Hold in 60 secs
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 10-20 secs unbroken Parallettes L-Sit Hold in 60 secs
Scale down as needed but aim for a shorter time (min 10 seconds) with a more difficult scale, rather than a longer time with an easier scale

L-Sits Scale (as in homework):
Rx: Straight legs, feet and legs together, feet @ 90° to hips or higher, shoulders behind wrists
Sc1: Bent knees or straight legs @ < 90°
Sc2: Bent knees in tuck position, shoulders in front of wrists


### KB OH Carry 2 x KB held overhead (6 mins)
2 rounds of:
Min 1: 60 secs unbroken KB OH Carry for metres & weight (light weight)
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 60 secs unbroken KB OH Carry for metres & weight (heavier weight to decide WOD weight)
KB OH Carry
Rx: 2 x ♀ 16kg, ♂ 24kg
Sc1: 2 x ♀ 12kg, ♂ 20kg
Sc2: 2 x ♀ 8kg, ♂ 16kg


## WOD: 2 x EMOM 20 mins total
(60 secs Work + 15 secs change over, add scores together for each movement for an overall total)

### 2 rounds of:

- Min 1: Max Strict Push-Ups
- Min 2: Max Strict Pull-Ups
- Min 3: Max secs Parallettes L-Sit Hold
- Min 4: Max SkiErg Metres
- Min 5: Max metres KB OH Carry
- Min 6: Max Rower Metres
- Min 7 & 8: REST

### NOTES:
These reps/seconds/metres do NOT have to be unbroken
Keep movements consistent with what we practiced during the class
Split large groups up.
Rx/advanced do as written, Scaled do Push-Ups, Pull-Ups, KBOHC, Rower, L-Sit, SkiErg.

coach_notes:
Snatch workout - ZT
https://youtu.be/rV1OsGZS_R0?si=kmpNc-SUd4sBevGW

Exercise Library
https://www.catalystathletics.com/exercises/

Early Arm Bend in the Snatch & Clean - Catalyst
https://www.catalystathletics.com/article/1904/Arm-Bend-in-the-Snatch-Clean-Its-Still-Not-the-Solution-to-/

Muscle Snatch from Power Position - Monroe Miller
https://www.facebook.com/reel/543227008885980

Womens 75kg 2018 European Weightlifting Championship - Lydia Valentin
https://youtu.be/Tug-UUMW7Ok

Mens 94kg 2018 European Weightlifting Championship
https://youtu.be/ySnqYSmAJ9o

### Purpose/Focus
The muscle snatch is helpful to learn, reinforce and strengthen the proper upper body mechanics of the turnover (third pull) of the snatch. 
The power position variation helps focus on vertical leg drive and better bar-body interaction to improve bar proximity. The abbreviated motion can also be used to reduce the contribution of the legs and place more emphasis on upper body strength. 
From power position relative to dip will allow more focus on proper position, balance and leg drive.


### Muscle Snatch from Power Position - Execution:
Start standing tall with the bar hanging at arms’ length in your snatch grip. 
Ensure even whole foot balance and brace your trunk. 
Bend at the knees only with a vertical trunk just as you would for a jerk, actively pushing the bar against your hips, and stop.
After a 1-2 second pause, drive up with the legs and complete a muscle snatch.
Focus on keeping the bar as close as possible, finishing with strong vertical leg drive, and punching vertically into the overhead position at the end of the turnover.
It can be helpful to think of the movement as a snatch high-pull with an added turnover of the bar afterward. This will reinforce the idea of driving vertically and lifting the elbows high and out before the turnover.
The only difference between a dip muscle snatch and a muscle snatch from power position is that there is a pause in the bottom of the dip in the muscle snatch from power position.

2 x Barbell Overhead Squats

title: Specialty

sections.content:
BLUE: Steven, NicoE, MichaelaE, Mimi,
RED: DanielB, Chris, Martina, MichaelaS,  
GREEN: Peter, Simon, Carole, Miriam,
YELLOW: Sergej, Sven, DanielS, SonjaH

* Team: Bumper Plate Burpee Relay
Team WOD Part 1: Bumper Plate Burpee Relay (12 mins)
Set up:
2 Teams line up together (pick randomly which teams go together) facing each other in a line from the 10 metre line to the opposite 10 metre line.
A stack of 16 weight plates are next to the 1st athlete (Each team has 10x 5kg & 6 x 10kg)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
The 1st athlete takes the 1st plate, places it on the ground in front of him and does a burpee with his hands on the plate. He then stands and passes the plate to the next athlete who does the same. This continues until all plates are restacked at the opposite end of the 10 metre line.
Plates can NOT be dropped, they must be placed down

* WB Bear Crawl
Set-up part A:
2 teams of 8 are randomly selected from the teams of 4
Each team begins behind the 10 metre line on opposite sides
32 assorted wallballs are placed in a line in the middle of the box on the 5 metre line
WBs per team: 3x 9kg, 2x 7kg, 3x 6kg, 2x 5kg, 1x 4kg, 1x 3kg (large) 2 x 3kg (small), 2 x 1kg (small)
Task:
On 3-2-1, Go!
1 player at a time must Bear Crawl forwards, and grab a WB WITHOUT USING THEIR HANDS!
The WB must remain underneath the body of the athlete at all times (they cannot kick or knee it forwards and chase after it)
They must turn around and Bear Crawl the WB back to their team
When the WB and athlete crosses the line, the next athlete can go
All Athletes must go at least once

Set-up Part B
Teams set up as in Part A
Prepare 2x 9kg, 2x 7kg, 2x 6kg, 1x 5kg & 1x 4kg Wallballs behind their 10 metre line
From the start to the end of their turn, teams of 2 must be in contact with each other at all times
The partnered athletes can NOT touch the Wallball with their hands at any time during the task
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams of 2 have to transport their Wallballs to the opposite side of the 10 metre line
When their WB is over the line they can return to their team remembering to remain in contact with each other until they cross the start line again

Part C (i)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Each team gets an image (Wallball Clown/Snowman xmas party 2025) which they have to recreate on the ground with the wallballs
Only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor
First to recreate the image wins

Part C (ii)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams have to recreate the image again, but this time, only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor
First to recreate the image wins

Part C (iii)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams have to recreate the image again, but this time, only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor and the clown/snowman MUST be upright
Players can hold/support the WBs in any way they see fit
First to recreate the exact image wins

Team WOD Part 3: * SkiErg + Synchro * Squat Jump
Set-up teams of 4:

Set 4 SkiErgs up in each of the 4 corners of the box facing inwards
Task:
On 3-2-1-Go!
A 1 min timer begins (30 secs rest/change over) and the athlete on the SkiErg begins. At the same time, the rest of the team perform Synchro Squat Jumps
The team must count their reps aloud and all team members must jump .
At the end of the 1 min work there is a 1 min rest
Write down Squat Jumps & Calories after every round
If any of the athletes cannot keep up, they are allowed to take up a Plank Position whilst the rest of the team completes the Squat Jumps.
Teams can only jump all together or if 1 athlete is in a Plank Position.
At least 2 people MUST perform the Squat Jumps
I planned to do 2 rounds but everyone was already done after 1!
Teams will be scored on Calories and Squat Jumps completed.

Team WOD Part 4:
Synchro:
* Push-up Strict
&
* DB/KB Floor to Overhead

Set-up:
All athletes choose a DB/KB that they can perform at least 10 reps unbroken

On 3-2-1-Go! a 5 min Timer begins
All athletes except 1 pick up their DB/KB and Snatch it overhead in synchronisation and then swap hands and repeat
Meanwhile, 1 athlete performs a Push-Up at the same time as the others do the F2OH
They repeat this as many times as they can until 1 or more athletes cannot continue
They can change the Push-Up athlete as many times as they like but Push-Ups & F2OH must always be performed together
Athletes who can do RX Push-Ups are not allowed to scale
Reps must be counted aloud

Task:
On 3-2-1, Go!

The WB must remain underneath the body of the athlete at all times (they cannot kick or knee it forwards and chase after it)
They must turn around and Bear Crawl the WB back to their team
When the WB and athlete crosses the line, the next athlete can go
All Athletes must go at least once

Set-up Part B
Teams set up as in Part A
Prepare 2x 9kg, 2x 7kg, 2x 6kg, 1x 5kg & 1x 4kg Wallballs behind their 10 metre line
From the start to the end of their turn, teams of 2 must be in contact with each other at all times
The partnered athletes can NOT touch the Wallball with their hands at any time during the task
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams of 2 have to transport their Wallballs to the opposite side of the 10 metre line
When their WB is over the line they can return to their team but on their return journey, only 2 feet may touch the floor at any time

Part C (1)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Each team gets an image (Wallball Clown/Snowman xmas party 2025) which they have to recreate on the ground with the wallballs 
Only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor
First to recreate the image wins

Part C (2)
Task:
On 3-2-1 GO!
Teams have to recreate the image again, but this time, only the 5kg Wallballs can touch the floor
First to recreate the image wins

Team WOD Part 3: SkiErg + Synchro Squat Jumps (20 mins)
Set-up teams of 4:
Set 4 SkiErgs up in each of the 4 corners of the box facing inwards
Task:
On 3-2-1-Go!
A 1 min timer begins and the athlete on the SkiErg begins. At the same time, the rest of the team perform Synchro Squat Jumps
The team must count their reps aloud and all team members must jump .
At the end of the 1 min work there is a 1 min rest
Write down Squat Jumps & Calories after every round
If any of the athletes cannot keep up, they are allowed to take up a Plank Position whilst the rest of the team completes the Squat Jumps.
Teams can only jump all together or if 1 athlete is in a Plank Position.
At least 2 people MUST perform the Squat Jumps
We will perform 2 rounds of this Workout but scores will be calculated after the first and second round separately
Teams will be scored on Calories and Squat Jumps completed.

Team WOD Part 5 (if needed) : Synchro Push-Ups & Alternating DB/KB Floor to Overhead (12 mins)
Set-up:
All athletes choose a DB/KB that they can perform at least 10 reps unbroken

On 3-2-1-Go!  a 5 min Timer begins
All athletes except 1 pick up their DB/KB and Snatch it overhead in synchronisation and then swap hands and repeat
Meanwhile, 1 athlete performs a Push-Up at the same time as the others do the F2OH
They repeat this as many times as they can until 1 or more athletes cannot continue
They can change the Push-Up athlete as many times as they like but Push-Ups & F2OH must always be performed together
Athletes who can do RX Push-Ups are not allowed to scale
Reps must be counted aloud

title: WOD

sections.content:
Gloria, Sabrina, Anna, Marion, Steven, DanielS, Robert, MichaelW (Marion's husband) Chris

Do the workout in the order as written.
Use a steady pace (don't go too fast at the beginning!)
Only scale if you absolutely have to!
If you can do at least 10 unbroken push-ups/sit-ups, do the workout Rx. Otherwise, scale these movements.
This is a long workout. Don't give up, take a break if you need one but finish the reps!
Even if you scale the moves, stay STRICT and make the last reps look EXACTLY like the first ones.

Push-Ups - Feet together, straight body & legs, hands under shoulders, elbows near to the body, chest touches the floor on EVERY rep!

Lunges- Step out far enough so that your knee stays above the ankle, NOT above the toes. (shins vertical) (nobody should scale these!)

Sit-Ups - Back straight, touch floor overhead and feet on return.

Airsquats - Hip below the knees on every rep. Stand straight up at the top so that the knees and hips are extended (nobody should scale these!)

* Hugger
* Hip Opener
* Hip Circle
* Walking RDL & Leg Swing
* Cat Camel
* Commando Crawl
* Elephant Walk
* Cossack Squat
* Scorpion Twist
* Walking Lunge & Twist
* Deep Squat Rig Support Stretch

This is a long workout. Don't give up, take a break if you need one but finish the reps!
Even if you scale the moves, stay STRICT and make the last reps look EXACTLY like the first ones.


**Push-Ups** - Feet together, straight body & legs, hands under shoulders, elbows near to the body, chest touches the floor on EVERY rep!

**Lunges**- Step out far enough so that your knee stays above the ankle, NOT above the toes. (shins vertical) (nobody should scale these!)

**Sit-Ups** - Back straight, touch floor overhead and feet on return. 

**Airsquats** - Hip below the knees on every rep. Stand straight up at the top so that the knees and hips are extended (nobody should scale these!)

coach_notes:
Single-Leg, 1-arm KB RDL - SU

PoP:
Perform the whole movement in a slow, controlled manner, any shaky or jerky movements must be corrected before increasing weight
Hold the KB in the opposite hand to the standing leg
Start with the standing leg slightly bent
Lower the KB down to the ground using a hip hinge without further bending the standing leg
Point the foot/toes of the raising leg to the ground to ensure that the hips do not rotate upwards
Keep the raising leg straight by squeezing the glutes
Keep the KB close to the body, don't reach forward
Touch the KB to the ground maintaining balance and control throughout
